audit_items.proof_trace: In `start()`, `_is_running` is set to `true` at line 36 before `_thread_group` (line 38) and `_timer_worker` (line 41) are allocated; if either `new` throws `std::bad_alloc` (exceptions are enabled — no -fno-exceptions in Makefile), `_is_running` stays `true` while the smart pointers remain null. The destructor calls `stop()`, which passes the `_is_running` guard at line 51 and then unconditionally dereferences `_timer_worker->stop()` (line 54) or `_thread_group->stop()` (line 55) on a null `shared_ptr`, producing undefined behavior / SIGSEGV.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker floods the RPC server with large requests to exhaust memory (OOM); when the server subsequently tries to instantiate a `TimeoutManager` (e.g., for a new `DynamicRpcChannelImpl`), `start()` sets `_is_running=true` then throws on `new TimerWorker()`, leaving `_timer_worker` null; the ensuing destructor call triggers the null dereference in `stop()`, crashing the server process (DoS).
